stem: In the figure below, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
solutionShortcut: 3-4-5 is the most common Pythagorean triple; recognize it directly.

stem: A right triangle has legs of length 6 and 8.
solutionStandard: \(c^2 = 6^2 + 8^2 = 100\), so \(c = 10\). Since \(10 < 11\), Quantity B is greater.
solutionShortcut: 6-8-10 is a \(2\times\) scaling of the 3-4-5 triple, so the hypotenuse is 10 directly.

stem: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of length {{13*k}} and one leg of length {{5*k}}. What is the length of the other leg?
solutionShortcut: 5-12-13 is a common Pythagorean triple; this is just that triple scaled by {{k}}.

stem: A 10-foot ladder leans against a vertical wall with its base 6 feet from the wall. How high up the wall does the ladder reach, in feet?
solutionStandard: The ladder is the hypotenuse: \(6^2 + h^2 = 10^2\), so \(h^2 = 64\), and \(h = 8\).
solutionShortcut: 6-8-10 is a \(2\times\) scaling of the 3-4-5 triple.

stem: Which of the following could be the side lengths of a right triangle? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: Check whether the square of the longest side equals the sum of squares of the other two: (3,4,5): \(25=9+16\) yes. (5,12,13): \(169=25+144\) yes. (2,3,4): \(16 \neq 13\), no. (6,8,10): \(100=36+64\) yes. (7,24,25): \(625=49+576\) yes. (1,1,1): \(1 \neq 2\), no.
solutionShortcut: Recognize 3-4-5, 5-12-13, and 7-24-25 as standard triples, and 6-8-10 as \(2\times\) the 3-4-5 triple; no computation needed for those four.

stem: A right triangle has two legs of equal length \(x\).
solutionStandard: \(c^2 = x^2 + x^2 = 2x^2\), so \(c = x\sqrt{2}\). The quantities are equal.

stem: A rectangle has a diagonal of length 15 and one side of length 9. What is the length of the other side?
solutionStandard: The diagonal splits the rectangle into a right triangle with legs 9 and the unknown side, and hypotenuse 15: \(9^2 + b^2 = 15^2\), so \(b^2 = 144\), and \(b = 12\).
solutionShortcut: 9-12-15 is a \(3\times\) scaling of the 3-4-5 triple.

stem: A right triangle has one leg of length 7 and a hypotenuse of length 25. What is the area of the triangle?
solutionShortcut: 7-24-25 is a standard Pythagorean triple, giving the other leg directly as 24.

stem: A right triangle has integer side lengths and a hypotenuse of 17. What is its perimeter?
solutionStandard: Look for integer legs \(a, b\) with \(a^2 + b^2 = 289\). The pair \((8, 15)\) works: \(64 + 225 = 289\). Perimeter \(= 8 + 15 + 17 = 40\).

stem: A right triangle has legs \(a\) and \(b\) (both positive) and hypotenuse \(c\).
solutionShortcut: Plug in a concrete case, e.g. the 3-4-5 triangle: \(c = 5\) and \(a + b = 7\), so Quantity B is greater. This matches the general result for any positive legs.

stem: A right triangle has legs of length {{8*k}} and {{15*k}}. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
solutionStandard: This is the 8-15-17 triple scaled by \({{k}}\): hypotenuse \(= {{17*k}}\).

stem: In the figure, what is the length of the hypotenuse?
solutionStandard: This is the 3-4-5 triple scaled by 2: \(x = 10\).

stem: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of length {{25*k}} and one leg of length {{7*k}}. What is the length of the other leg?
solutionStandard: This is the 7-24-25 triple scaled by \({{k}}\): the other leg is \({{24*k}}\).

stem: A rectangular TV screen has width 24 inches and height 18 inches. What is the length of the diagonal, in inches?
solutionStandard: 18 and 24 are \(6\times\) the 3-4-5 triple: diagonal \(= 6\times5=30\).

stem: A right triangle has legs of length {{3*k}} and {{4*k}}.
solutionStandard: This is the 3-4-5 triple scaled by \({{k}}\), so the hypotenuse is \({{5*k}}\), always exactly 1 more than Quantity B.

stem: Which of the following could be the side lengths of a right triangle? Select all that apply.
solutionStandard: (A) is \(3\times\) the 3-4-5 triple. (B) is a standard triple. (D) is \(2\times\) the 5-12-13 triple. (C): \(16+25=41\neq36\), no. (E) is equilateral, not right (all angles \(60^\circ\)).

stem: A right triangle has one leg of length {{7*k}} and a hypotenuse of length {{25*k}}. What is the area of the triangle?
solutionStandard: This is the 7-24-25 triple scaled by \({{k}}\), so the other leg is \({{24*k}}\). Area \(= \frac{1}{2}({{7*k}})({{24*k}}) = {{84*k*k}}\).

stem: A right triangle has integer side lengths, a hypotenuse of 25, and one leg of length 15. What is its perimeter?
solutionStandard: 15-20-25 is \(5\times\) the 3-4-5 triple, so the other leg is 20. Perimeter \(= 15+20+25=60\).

stem: A right triangle has integer side lengths and a hypotenuse of {{17*k}}, with the shortest leg being {{8*k}}. What is its perimeter?
solutionStandard: This is the 8-15-17 triple scaled by \({{k}}\): perimeter \(= {{8*k}}+{{15*k}}+{{17*k}} = {{40*k}}\).

stem: A right triangle has legs \(a\) and \(b\) with \(a+b=14\), and hypotenuse \(c\).
solutionStandard: \(c^2=a^2+b^2=(a+b)^2-2ab=196-2ab\). When \(a=b=7\) (most "balanced"), \(ab=49\) is maximized, giving \(c^2=98\) and \(c=7\sqrt2\approx9.9<10\). When the legs are very unequal (e.g. \(a=13,b=1\)), \(c^2=170\) and \(c\approx13>10\). Since \(c\) can be either less than or greater than 10, the relationship cannot be determined.
